name: Direction Selectivity
short_description: Neural responses that depend on the direction of a moving or spreading stimulus.
detailed_description: Direction selectivity is a functional property in which a neuron's firing rate depends on the direction of a moving stimulus or the spatial sequence of synaptic activation. This project studies how the internal electrophysiological properties of a single retinal ganglion cell shape its direction-selective response, with the goal of matching a target angle-to-AP-frequency tuning curve.
